title: galium umbellatum var. scabrifolium
intro: Galium umbellatum var. scabrifolium, also known as a variety of the common St. Michael's spikenard, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing nausea and stomach cramps, as well as soothing menstrual cramps and other related symptoms. Herbal preparations of Galium umbellatum var. scabrifolium can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and alleviate various health conditions.
